creator: pen@lysator.liu.se
text:
Created attachment 251077
Patch to enable FreeBSD ZFS/NFS4 ACLs to rsync 3.3.0

This patch is a work (very much) in progress. It enables FreeBSD support for NFS4/ZFS ACLs and seems to work. Minimal testing so far.

I've been annoyed for a long time with the fact that the Rsync ACLs support is really ... not so portable. And when it supports NFS4/ZFS ACLs it hasn't been able to send ACLs between different OS systems.

This patch adds a start of a way to send NFS4/ZFS/SMB/MacOS ACLs portably between FreeBSD/Solaris/MacOS/Linux (and possibly Windows) systems in the future by converting the NFS4 ACLs into a "transport neutral" format while being sent over the wire. Currently only implemented for FreeBSD but I'll try to add in support for Solaris, MacOS and Linux in the near future since I already have code that handles those systems in my "acltool" utility.

(And yes, this code should to into upstream but I thought I'd mention it here since people are bound to search for rsync and acl problems here too)
